nameMeanings.name: Prested
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Middle English plei(e) ‘play, game, performance’ + stede ‘place, location’ (Old English plegstede, plægstede). The surname may be for someone who lived near a place for physical or theatrical play, or for someone from a place so named, such as Chapel Plaster in Box (Wilts), which is recorded as Pleystede in 1268. Compare Plastow .

variants.name: Plastow
meanings: English: locative name from Middle English plei(e) ‘play, game, performance’ + stou(e) ‘place, location’ (Old English plegstōw, * plægstōw). The surname may be for someone who lived near a place for physical or theatrical play, or for someone from any of numerous places so named, such as Plaistow (Essex, Surrey, Gloucs, Devon, Sussex), Playstow (Herts), Pleystowe (Surrey), or Plastow (Hants). Compare Plested .

variants.name: Plaisted
meanings: English (mainly Kent): topographic name from Middle English plei(e) ‘play, game, performance’ + stede ‘place, location’ (Old English plegstede, plægstede). The surname may be for someone who lived near a place for physical or theatrical play, or from a place so named, such as Chapel Plaster in Wiltshire. Compare Plaster 2.

variants.name: Plaster
meanings: English and North German: metonymic occupational name for a plasterer, from Middle English, Middle Low German plaster (from Latin emplastrum ‘(wound) plaster’ (originally a paste), from Greek emplastron, a derivative of emplassein ‘to shape or form’; the term was carried over into building terminology to mean ‘bonding agent’).

English: variant from a common pronunciation of Plastow, a habitational name from any of various places called Plaistow (in East London, Derbyshire, Sussex, and elsewhere), from Old English plegestōw ‘place where people gather for sport or play’. This can also be a variant of Plaisted (through interchangeable use of the Old English elements stōw and stede, both meaning ‘place’, in earlier times).

variants.name: Playle
meanings: English (Essex): apparently a habitational name from an unidentified place called in Old English plega ‘play, sport’ + hyll ‘hill’. Compare Plaster 2.

fr.lifeSummary: Prested a eu au moins 1 fils.

ja.lifeSummary: Prestedは少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Prested(은)는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: У Prested был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Prested tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
